emails.subject: Delivery Waiting!!!!!!!
emails.body_text:
Hey!

Seriously need your help right now - one of my drivers has been sitting at your Houston gate for 20 minutes and there's NO security to let him in! 

It's already 77 degrees out here and his cargo temp is reading 86F. We're losing time and I'm getting really worried about the integrity of everything in the back, but most importantly my driver's safety.

Can you PLEASE get someone out there ASAP? I know it's early but this is critical.

Call me when you get this!

Ty

emails.subject: Pest Contamination
emails.body_text:
Hi,

We have a serious problem with the Texpal delivery that just came in this morning.

While unloading the pallets, I discovered rodent droppings on at least 4-5 of them. The contamination looks recent and is definitely a health hazard.

I've stopped the unloading process and quarantined the affected pallets. The rest of the shipment is on hold until we figure out next steps.

We need to document this immediately and decide whether to reject the entire shipment or just the contaminated pallets. I've taken photos for our records.

Sarah Kim
Warehouse Associate (Senior)
Houston Distribution Center

emails.subject: Upcoming Server Update
emails.body_text:
Hey,

Just wanted to give you a heads up about something I heard through the grapevine.

Apparently IT is doing some major server updates later this week and there's a chance some files on the shared drive might get wiped or corrupted during the migration. I know they're supposed to have backups, but... you know how that goes sometimes.

Anyway, I'd strongly recommend saving a personal copy of the inventory_master.xlsx file, just to be safe. That thing would be a nightmare to rebuild from scratch if something goes wrong.

This isn't official or anything, just me looking out for you. Better safe than sorry!

Hannah

Hannah Brooks
Inventory Analyst
PrairieStar

emails.subject: Freezer Temp
emails.body_text:
Hi there,

I'm really worried about something and I'm not sure what to do. I checked the freezer temperature in zone H-A this morning and it was reading -28 degrees. 

I know that seems really cold but I'm concerned it might be malfunctioning or something? I'm worried about all the stock in there getting damaged and if we miss orders because of this it would be a disaster. I've only been here a few months and I really don't want to mess anything up.

Should I be concerned about this? I mean, the gauge seemed to be working fine and everything, but -28 just seems extreme to me. I double-checked and my thermometer was definitely reading accurately - I had it set to C when I took the measurement.

I'm probably overthinking this. I just want to make sure we're not risking any product integrity or customer orders.?

Thanks so much,

Kevin White
Warehouse Associate
Houston Distribution Center
